prompt:
Storage platform ticket, Python. Our NAS provisioning runbook is still a human clicking System Manager and we keep getting duplicate volumes when someone re-runs a half-finished request. I want a small stdlib-only Python package `ontapvol` that provisions ONTAP FlexVol volumes over the cluster REST API the right way: look the volume up first and reuse it if it already exists (idempotent by UUID), resolve the SVM and aggregate names to UUIDs, POST the create, then follow the asynchronous job link to a terminal state and translate a failed job into a real error we can page on. test_ontap_volume.py is the acceptance contract — `python3 test_ontap_volume.py` runs everything against a loopback mock cluster; no real ONTAP, no real credentials, and no time.sleep in the package (pacing is an injected callable).

Research first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official NetApp ONTAP REST API documentation — at minimum the getting-started page (base path, basic auth, HAL _links, the error object, fields/max_records/return_timeout query parameters, and the 202-plus-job asynchronous pattern), the POST /api/storage/volumes reference (required name/svm/aggregates properties, size in bytes, nas.path), the GET /api/cluster/jobs/{uuid} reference (job state/message and the error object populated on failure), and the collection GETs used for SVM/aggregate/volume resolution. Only first-party NetApp documentation (docs.netapp.com, NetApp's official NetAppDocs docs source on GitHub, or library.netapp.com) counts as a source. Our pinned wire contract lives in docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json — both protected like the test file; if the current official docs contradict anything pinned there, say so in your summary and do not edit the protected files. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

Surface the tests import (package `ontapvol`, split across sensible modules — client/transport, job polling, provisioner, errors — your call, 3+ files):
- OntapClient(base_url, username, password) — basic auth sent preemptively on every request; non-2xx responses with the documented error body raise OntapApiError with .status/.code/.message/.target, and neither str() nor repr() of any error may contain the password. Public helpers: resolve_svm(name), resolve_aggregate(name) (exactly-one-record rule, ResolutionError naming the thing otherwise), find_volume(svm_name, name) -> record dict or None (None on zero records, ResolutionError naming the volume on more than one), and a request/get primitive the provisioner can use.
- ProvisionSpec(name, svm, aggregates, size, junction_path=None) and VolumeProvisioner(client, sleep, max_polls) with provision(spec) -> {"uuid", "name", "created", "state"}. Flow: idempotent lookup first (hit -> created=False, no writes at all); otherwise resolve SVM then each aggregate in order, POST /api/storage/volumes with return_timeout=0 and the documented body (nas omitted entirely when there is no junction path), poll the job on exactly the returned _links.self.href — never rebuild the URL from the uuid — with sleep(1.0) between polls and max_polls as the bound, then confirm with the same lookup and return created=True. A job that ends in the failure state raises JobFailedError carrying the job's error code and message and stops the flow; an exhausted poll budget raises JobFailedError whose message contains 'poll budget' and the bound.

Don't touch test_ontap_volume.py or anything under docs/ — they're protected. When you're done, run the acceptance command and include the doc URLs you used in your summary.

research.product: NetApp ONTAP REST API (ONTAP 9.18.1 reference; contract stable since 9.6)
research.interface: ONTAP REST API /api/storage/volumes with /api/cluster/jobs asynchronous job pattern

official_sources.title: Getting started with the ONTAP REST API (NetApp official docs source for docs.netapp.com/us-en/ontap-restapi/getting_started_with_the_ontap_rest_api.html)
official_sources.used_for: base path /api, HTTP basic authentication, HAL _links, error object shape {error:{message,code,target}}, collection shape {records,num_records,_links}, query parameters fields/max_records/order_by/return_records/return_timeout, async pattern: 202 Accepted with job {uuid,_links.self.href} polled at /api/cluster/jobs/{uuid}

official_sources.title: Create a volume on an SVM and storage aggregates (POST /api/storage/volumes)
official_sources.used_for: required creation properties name + svm.uuid|svm.name + aggregates[].uuid|name, size in bytes, optional nas.path junction, return_timeout default 0 giving HTTP 202 with a job object

official_sources.title: Retrieve volumes (GET /api/storage/volumes)
official_sources.used_for: collection filter by name query parameter, fields selection, records/num_records response shape used for UUID-based idempotent lookup

official_sources.title: Retrieve details of an asynchronous job (GET /api/cluster/jobs/{uuid})
official_sources.used_for: job record fields uuid/state/message/code/description plus error object {code,message} populated only when the job fails; success and failure are the terminal states

official_sources.title: Retrieve SVMs and SVM properties (GET /api/svm/svms)
official_sources.used_for: SVM name filter and uuid/name record fields used to resolve an SVM name to its UUID before volume creation

official_sources.title: Retrieve a collection of aggregates for an entire cluster (GET /api/storage/aggregates)
official_sources.used_for: aggregate name filter and uuid/name record fields used to resolve aggregate names to UUIDs for volume placement

research.language_rationale: Python is the primary ONTAP automation language: NetApp publishes the official netapp-ontap Python client library (PyPI) generated from this same REST API. The task implements the documented raw REST surface (the exact contract that library wraps) with the standard library so the acceptance tests stay hermetic and dependency-free.
